method: Robustness hardening of the E072 libation-order cross-site positive. SAME metric + SAME null as E072 (frozen): order-consistency of co-occurring word-form pairs (anonymous sign-tuples, len>=2); within-inscription word-order shuffle null (preserves each inscription's word multiset -> invariant testable/cross-site pair sets; destroys only order); testable pairs >=2 occ; cross-site pairs >=2 sites; consistency=max(n_plus,n_minus)/tot; C_cross=mean over cross-site pairs; A_cross=# cross pairs whose dominant order agrees in every site. Robustness = recompute the SAME statistic on leave-one-out subsets (LOSO: drop each libation site with >=5 inscriptions; LHIO: drop the top-carrier inscription, then top-2, top-3). SIGNAL-LOSS vs POWER-LOSS discipline: a leave-out with n_cross<3 is UNDERPOWERED (not fragility); only a powered leave-out (n_cross>=3) that loses significance (perm p>0.05) or collapses C_cross toward the null is FRAGILE. Effective-n (Art. VIII) = #distinct carrier inscriptions / #distinct carrier sites vs raw pair count. >=1000 draws, one-sided perm p, add-one smoothed. PC SYNTHETIC. L3 ONLY: no reading, no meaning.

effective_n.note: 10 raw cross-site pairs but only 8 distinct carrier inscriptions across 6 sites; the hub (IOZa2, Iouktas) is an endpoint of ALL 10 pairs; the top-3 carriers (IOZa2, TLZa1, KOZa1) account for 10+6+6=22 pair-endpoints. Pairs sharing the hub are NOT independent evidence.

positive_control.detect_fragile_detail: Hub-concentrated synthetic corpus (8 canon inscriptions at S0+S1): full C_cross=0.938 (n_cross=16); dropping hub site S0 -> n_cross=4, C_cross=0.750, perm_p=0.5175 (non-significant). Test correctly FLAGS concentration.
positive_control.detect_robust_detail: Broadly-spread synthetic corpus (20 canon inscriptions across 6 sites): full C_cross=0.892; dropping top site -> n_cross=13, C_cross=0.958, perm_p=0.0010. Test correctly PASSES as ROBUST.
positive_control.power_detail: Over 20 replicate pairs (fragile vs robust corpus), the leave-out test correctly classified the fragile corpus's hub-drop as FRAGILE/UNDERPOWERED and the robust corpus's top-drop as ROBUST in 20/20 = 1.00 of replicates.

key_findings: E072's libation-order cross-site positive is REAL and SITE-BROAD: the canonical order survives EVERY leave-one-site-out (LOSO) drop ROBUSTLY, including dropping Iouktas (the hub site) which keeps n_cross=3 (powered, at the floor) with C_cross=1.0 and perm_p=0.0455 (still significant). Dropping Zakros/Palaikastro/Syme leaves n_cross=9-10 with C_cross=1.0, p=0.0005. The order signal is NOT carried by any single site.

BUT the cross-site BREADTH is INSCRIPTION-CONCENTRATED: the 10 raw cross-site pairs are carried by only 8 distinct inscriptions across 6 sites (effective-n << 10), and the hub inscription IOZa2 (Iouktas) is an endpoint of ALL 10 pairs. The top-3 carriers (IOZa2, TLZa1/Troullos, KOZa1/Kophinas) account for the overwhelming majority of pair-endpoints. Pairs sharing the hub are NOT independent evidence.

LEAVE-HUB-INSCRIPTION-OUT confirms the concentration: dropping the hub alone (top1=IOZa2) SURVIVES (n_cross=4, C_cross=1.0, perm_p=0.0080, ROBUST) — the hub inscription alone does NOT carry the signal. But dropping the hub PLUS one partner (top2=IOZa2+TLZa1) leaves n_cross=3 (powered) with C_cross=1.0 but perm_p~0.056 (non-significant); dropping top-3 leaves n_cross=2 (UNDERPOWERED). The breadth is carried by ~3 inscriptions.

CRITICAL DISCIPLINE DISTINCTION (signal-loss vs power-loss): in EVERY powered leave-out C_cross stays at 1.0 (the order signal itself NEVER collapses toward the null ~0.74). The top2 non-significance is PURELY a power-loss at n_cross=3 (too few pairs to reach p<=0.05), NOT a C_cross collapse. This is why the verdict is QUALIFIED (narrow breadth) rather than FRAGILE (signal collapse). The canonical order is intact wherever it is observed; what is narrow is HOW MANY independent inscriptions carry it.

POSITIVE CONTROL (synthetic) PASSED with power_est=1.00: the machinery correctly FLAGS a hub-concentrated synthetic order as fragile/qualified on hub-drop (n_cross 16->4, C_cross 0.938->0.750, p->0.52) and correctly PASSES a broadly-spread synthetic order as ROBUST on top-site-drop (n_cross 13, p=0.001). The test CAN distinguish concentration-driven from broadly-supported order at the observed scale.

NET: E072 stands as a REAL but NARROW cross-site positive. The canonical order is genuine (survives all site-drops and the hub-inscription-drop alone), but its cross-site breadth rests on a small effective-n (8 inscriptions / 6 sites, with ~3 inscriptions carrying most of the weight). E072 should be read as 'a canonical order shared across a SMALL set of libation inscriptions spanning several sites', not as a broad corpus-wide formula. This is an append-only QUALIFICATION of E072, not a superseding correction.
successor_hypotheses: E076: Expand the carrier base — are there additional libation inscriptions (e.g. single-site or low-frequency) that, if included via a relaxed co-occurrence threshold, would broaden the effective-n and test whether the canonical order is more widely attested than the strict >=2-occurrence pair rule reveals?

E077: Test whether the canonical order generalizes beyond the 3 hub+partner inscriptions (IOZa2, TLZa1, KOZa1) to OTHER religious supports (libation tables, altars, cult objects) — does the same ordered sequence appear on non-stone-vessel libation supports, which would broaden the cross-site breadth?

E078: Partial-order decomposition — is the canonical order a single total order (linear 'liturgy') or a partial order (DAG of precedence constraints)? If partial, the independent precedence constraints may each be supported by different (independent) inscription subsets, recovering breadth even when the full linear order is narrow.

E079: Stratified robustness — within the 3 carrier inscriptions, are the shared forms drawn from the same time/stratum, or do they span periods? A cross-temporal canonical order would strengthen the 'real formula' reading; a single-period cluster would suggest a localized tradition.

E080: Hub-inscription content analysis (L3 structural only) — does IOZa2 contain a distinctive structural signature (e.g. the longest form-sequence, a unique sign-cluster) that explains why it is the universal endpoint, or is its hub-role a pure frequency artifact of co-occurring with many distinct forms?

E081: Power-aware re-test with a larger libation corpus — if future corpus additions increase the carrier-inscription count, does the LHIO top2 leave-out cross back into significance (confirming the current non-significance is power-loss, not signal-loss)?
non_circular: SAME metric + SAME null as E072 (frozen, not re-derived). Anonymous word-forms only (sign tuples, len>=2); no reading, no phonetic value, no meaning. Order = pure stream position (first occurrence). The within-inscription word-order shuffle null preserves each inscription's word multiset exactly, so on each subset the testable/cross-site pair sets are invariant under the null — only the ORDER signal changes. Robustness recomputes the SAME statistic on leave-one-out subsets; no new statistic is invented. SIGNAL-LOSS vs POWER-LOSS is distinguished mechanically: n_cross<3 => UNDERPOWERED (power-loss, not fragility); only a powered leave-out (n_cross>=3) with perm p>0.05 OR C_cross collapsing toward the null is FRAGILE (signal-loss). In this epoch, EVERY powered leave-out keeps C_cross=1.0 (no collapse); the single non-significant powered leave-out (LHIO top2, n_cross=3, p~0.056) is a power-loss at narrow breadth, not a signal collapse. Effective-n (Art. VIII) is reported as #distinct carrier inscriptions (8) / #distinct carrier sites (6) vs the raw 10 pairs, with the explicit note that pairs sharing the hub inscription are NOT independent evidence. PC is fully synthetic. L3 only.
deviations: Used 2000 permutation draws for baseline and each LOSO/LHIO leave-out (>=1000 required); used 5000 draws to confirm the LHIO top2 perm_p stability (0.054-0.066 across 10 seeds, consistently >0.05).

PC power arm used 500 draws per replicate within the 20-replicate discrimination test (to keep runtime tractable); the discrimination power was 20/20=1.00, well above the 0.5 floor. The detect-fragile and detect-robust arms used 1000 draws.

The LHIO 'top2' and 'top3' drops are defined by carrier-inscription pair-count ranking (IOZa2=10, TLZa1=6, KOZa1=6); TLZa1 and KOZa1 are tied at 6 and were taken in corpus-index order. This is a mechanical tie-break, not a value judgment.
This epoch QUALIFIES E072 (append-only): it does not modify E072's verdict or numbers, only adds a robustness/erratum note that the cross-site breadth is narrower than the raw pair count suggests.
